steps.title: Read the context for 'consideration'
steps.content: The passage says Natalie feared there would "never be time to approach college with appropriate consideration, to form a workable personality to take along." The phrase "form a workable personality to take along" tells us consideration here is a deliberate, thoughtful mental process — not an emotion or an attitude toward others.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Admiration': Admiration means regard for someone else's qualities; nothing in the passage involves admiring anything. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Indecision': Indecision is the inability to choose; Natalie is worried about not having time to prepare, not about being unable to decide. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Reflection': Reflection is careful, deliberate thought — exactly the internal preparation needed to "form a workable personality." ✓
  • Choice D — 'Kindness': Kindness is a disposition toward others; the passage focuses on Natalie's internal preparation, not her behavior toward people. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
